reasoning: There is currently no human vaccine for Clostridium perfringens food poisoning, nor are there any registered human clinical trials as of 2026. The disease is generally mild and self-limiting, which significantly reduces the clinical need and financial incentive for vaccine development in humans (although veterinary vaccines for livestock exist). Because vaccine development and approval typically take 8-15 years, it is virtually impossible to have an approved vaccine mapped to Wikipedia by 2031. Any positive resolution in the near term would likely be due to temporary Wikipedia vandalism or errors, which carries a baseline probability of roughly 2%. For the most distant date (2036), there is a very small non-zero chance that early-stage research (such as recent bivalent or mRNA candidates) progresses to a successful vaccine, which brings the probability up to 5%.
